race.name: Stowe Derby
race.tagline: Twenty kilometers of pure descent down Mount Mansfield — America's oldest ski race, where 2,700 feet of vertical drop separates the bold from the bruised since 1945.

climate.description: Late February in Vermont's Green Mountains brings variable conditions. Temperatures range from -15°C to 0°C. Mount Mansfield's summit can be Arctic-cold while the valley finish is significantly warmer. Snow cover is generally good at altitude but lower sections can be thin or icy. Wind on exposed mountain sections adds serious chill.
challenges: Extreme temperature differential between summit start and valley finish
Ice and hardpack on steep descents
Wind exposure on upper mountain sections
Variable snow coverage on lower-elevation trails

course.primary: A unique downhill cross-country course starting near the summit of Mount Mansfield (Vermont's highest peak at 1,339m) and descending 823 vertical meters to Stowe village. The course uses a mix of ski trails, logging roads, and backcountry routes. Technical descents dominate — this is not a typical XC marathon but a gravity-assisted adventure through the Green Mountains.
course.surface: Mixed — groomed trails, logging roads, and backcountry routes
course.grooming: Minimal — some sections groomed, many are natural backcountry
features: 2,700 feet (823m) of vertical descent — nearly all downhill
Starts near the summit of Mount Mansfield, Vermont's highest peak
One of America's oldest continuously-run ski races (since 1945)
Mix of groomed trails, logging roads, and backcountry terrain
Legendary steep descents that test nerve and skill

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum technicality score for the unique downhill XC format — 823m of vertical descent demands advanced skill. Prestige 4 reflects 80+ years of history as one of America's oldest ski races. Small field and limited grooming constrain overall score but add to the cult appeal.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 promotes one tier but cannot enter TIER 1 — confirmed TIER 2

history.summary: Founded in 1945, the Stowe Derby is one of the oldest ski races in the United States. The concept is unique: a 20km descent from near the summit of Mount Mansfield to Stowe village, dropping 2,700 vertical feet. Originally conceived as a test of all-around skiing ability, the Derby predates the modern separation of alpine and Nordic disciplines. It remains a beloved Vermont institution and a rite of passage for adventurous New England skiers.
notable_facts: One of the oldest continuously-run ski races in America — since 1945
Unique downhill XC format — 2,700 feet of vertical descent
Predates the modern separation of alpine and Nordic skiing
Mount Mansfield is Vermont's highest peak at 4,393 feet (1,339m)
Course uses a mix of ski trails, logging roads, and backcountry routes
Considered a rite of passage for New England cross-country skiers

videos.curation_reason: Recent race coverage with detailed course description, racer interviews, and specific race atmosphere details including the 2800-foot vertical drop through 20 kilometers.

videos.title: 2022 Stowe Derby: Short, Fast, and Icy!
videos.description: It was a freezing cold, icy day in Vermont for the 2022 Stowe Derby. It was 50 degrees the day before, but the high on race day was 10. Conditions were firm, fast, and treacherous. Owing to some apparent grooming challenges on the lower course , there was a last minute change in the course. The planned 20k race ended at the base of Mansfield at the Stowe Nordic Center parking lot, around 7k total. Starting off not knowing this, it came as quite a surprise to everyone to cross the finish line minutes after starting. A few determined racers actually completed the race all the way into Stow to the traditional finish line. Hats off to those for braving the ice and completing the race! Our own post-race recon of skiing the course from the traditional finish line up found icy but passable conditions up until the trail entered the forest off of the Stowe Recreation path.
videos.curation_reason: Detailed first-person race experience focusing on challenging icy conditions and course changes, providing valuable insight into race conditions and course logistics.
